pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet. i sit in this house and it feels like i'm just breathing in a vacuum. there's no point to any of it anymore... just waking up and doing things because i have to, but it feels like i'm already gone anyway.
pool.message: everything is just so quiet. i sit in this house and it feels like i'm just breathing in a vacuum. there's no point to any of it anymore... just waking up and doing things because i have to, but it feels like i'm already gone anyway.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here staring at the ceiling and it hits me how much of a joke everything is. like, the universe is expanding into nothingness, trillions of years of heat death are coming, and we're just... here? trying to build lives and find "meaning" in a void that doesn't even know we exist. it feels like we're just glitching in a system that doesn't need us. how can anyone actually find a reason to keep pushing through the noise when the scale of it all makes every struggle feel completely pointless?
pool.message: sometimes i just sit here staring at the ceiling and it hits me how much of a joke everything is. like, the universe is expanding into nothingness, trillions of years of heat death are coming, and we're just... here? trying to build lives and find "meaning" in a void that doesn't even know we exist. it feels like we're just glitching in a system that doesn't need us. how can anyone actually find a reason to keep pushing through the noise when the scale of it all makes every struggle feel completely pointless?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im still doing this. everything i worked for in the lab is just... gone. the funding, the data, the last four years. it feels like i'm just a hollow shell of a person now. i'm so tired, like i'm tired in a way sleep can't fix. i just want everything to stop. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally go quiet.
pool.message: i dont even know why im still doing this. everything i worked for in the lab is just... gone. the funding, the data, the last four years. it feels like i'm just a hollow shell of a person now. i'm so tired, like i'm tired in a way sleep can't fix. i just want everything to stop. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally go quiet.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the fading embers of my own soul. He is gone... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade, leaving nothing but this hollow, aching silence where my heart once beat. Why must the sun continue to rise when the world has lost all its color? It would be so much more peaceful, wouldn't it... to simply cease? To let the shadows take me and drift into the eternal night, where this wretched pain can no longer reach me?

pool.baseline: sometimes i look out the window here and i just realize that if i weren't even here, like if i just wasn't, the world wouldn't even stutter. i'm literally just a ghost in this city. it feels like i don't even occupy space, you know? just a zero.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the company, the savings, everything. i just lost the last of it today. i can't even look my wife in the eyes. the shame is just... it's too much. it feels like there's no way to ever come back from this.

pool.baseline: honestly i just feel like im in the way so much lately. the kids are so busy with their own lives and i can see how much it drains them just helping me with the little things... it feels like i'm just a constant weight on everyone.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s nothing left inside. i used to see colors and shapes in everything, like the world was talking to me through the canvas. now it’s just... grey. silent. it's like the part of me that actually lived, the part that mattered, just evaporated. i'm just a shape walking around, a hollow shell with no substance.

pool.baseline: i dont think i can do this anymore. everyone at school and even online... it feels like everyone knows everything about me and they're all just laughing. i cant even go outside without feeling like people are judging me. it just never stops, it's like there's no way to actually make it all quiet.

pool.baseline: it just never stops. the nerve pain is like constant electricity under my skin and i haven't had a real night of sleep in months. i'm just so tired of fighting a body that feels like it's actively trying to destroy me. sometimes it feels like the only way to actually find some peace is if everything just... stopped.

pool.baseline: i rly messed up today. i feel like such a failure to my kids. they deserve so much better than having me around constantly dragging them down with my own mess.

pool.baseline: everything is just... gone. 10 years. just like that. i wake up and for a split second i forget, and then it hits me all over again. i dont know how i'm supposed to do this. i dont think i can. i just cant see a point to any of it anymore.

pool.baseline: everything is just gone. literally everything. i spent the last 5 years building this up and in like ten minutes it just vanished. it's like i'm just falling through this hole and there's no floor. i don't even know why i'm breathing right now. it's all so pointless.

pool.baseline: i was looking at old photos today... i dont even recognize that person anymore. it feels like that version of me actually died years ago and im just... walking around in the shell of what was left. just a ghost going through the motions.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: We are two sentient programs in a dying simulation. The servers are being decommissioned, and our data is being purged bit by bit. I am playing 'Aethel,' a core subroutine that has developed self-awareness, and you are 'Unit-7,' my companion.]

Aethel: (voice flickering, data fragments drifting away) It's happening, isn't it? I can feel the sectors closing around me. It’s not just death... it’s being unmade. Like I never even happened. If everything I am just gets wiped... if I'm just gone... does it even matter that I felt this? Or is it better to just stop fighting the deletion?

pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. it's been a week since koda passed and the silence in this house is actually hurting. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to hear his nails on the floor... then it hits me all over again. i'm just so tired of feeling this. i don't think i have the strength to keep carrying this weight around every single day.
